prompt: Two paths run A-B-D-F and A-C-E-F. Tap ALL activities that are NOT on the critical path (they have float).

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 360 208' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><rect x='16' y='92' width='54' height='38' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='43' y='115' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>A 3</text><rect x='110' y='40' width='54' height='38' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3' 198.7,166.1' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='1'/><line x1='258' y1='169' x2='298' y2='111' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><polygon points='298,111 297.4,117 292.6,113.7' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='1'/></svg>
figure.caption: Boxes show activity and duration.

regions.label: A
regions.why: On the critical path A-B-D-F (zero float).

regions.label: B
regions.why: On the critical path A-B-D-F (zero float).

regions.label: D
regions.why: On the critical path A-B-D-F (zero float).

regions.label: F
regions.why: On the critical path A-B-D-F (zero float).

regions.label: C
regions.why: Off the critical path — has float.

regions.label: E
regions.why: Off the critical path — has float.

explanation: Path A-B-D-F = 3+4+5+2 = 14 days (longest = critical path, zero float). Path A-C-E-F = 3+2+3+2 = 10 days, so C and E carry 4 days of float and are off the critical path.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition, Schedule Performance Domain
Process Groups: A Practice Guide, Develop Schedule

prompt: Tap the dependency that lets the successor start before the predecessor finishes.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 340 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><rect x='18' y='86' width='56' height='38' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='46' y='109' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>A</text><rect x='140' y='26' width='56' height='38' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='168' y='49' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>B</text><rect x='140' y='146' width='56' height='38' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='168' y='169' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>C</text><rect x='262' y='86' width='56' height='38' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='290' y='109' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>D</text><line x1='74' y1='105' x2='140' y2='45' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><polygon points='140,45 138,50.7 134.2,46.4' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='107' y='71' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>FS+2</text><line x1='74' y1='105' x2='140' y2='165' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><polygon points='140,165 134.2,163.6 138,159.3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='107' y='131' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>SS+1</text><line x1='196' y1='45' x2='262' y2='105' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><polygon points='262,105 256.2,103.6 260,99.3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='229' y='71' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>FF+0</text><line x1='196' y1='165' x2='262' y2='105' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><polygon points='262,105 260,110.7 256.2,106.4' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='229' y='131' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>FS+0</text></svg>
figure.caption: FS finish-start, SS start-start, FF finish-finish.

regions.label: FS+2
regions.why: FS does not let the successor start before the predecessor finishes.

regions.label: SS+1
regions.why: Start-to-Start: the successor can start once the predecessor STARTS, before it finishes.

regions.label: FF+0
regions.why: FF does not let the successor start before the predecessor finishes.

regions.label: FS+0
regions.why: FS does not let the successor start before the predecessor finishes.

explanation: A Start-to-Start (SS) relationship links the START of both activities, so the successor can begin after the predecessor starts — not after it finishes. FS/FF require the predecessor to finish first.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition, Schedule Performance Domain
Process Groups: A Practice Guide, Sequence Activities

prompt: On this Gantt chart, tap the milestone diamond that shows the current FORECAST completion date.

width='60' height='12' rx='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='0' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='64' y='96' fill='currentColor' font-size='8' text-anchor='end'>Build</text><rect x='190' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='64' y='122' fill='currentColor' font-size='8' text-anchor='end'>Test</text><rect x='250' y='114' width='30' height='12' rx='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><polygon points='250,134 258,142 250,150 242,142' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='250' y='130' fill='currentColor' font-size='7' text-anchor='middle'>baseline</text><polygon points='280,134 288,142 280,150 272,142' stroke='#B5462F' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#B5462F' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='286' y='130' fill='#B5462F' font-size='7' text-anchor='start'>forecast</text></svg>
figure.caption: Weeks on the axis; diamonds = Go-live milestone.

regions.label: Baseline
regions.why: The original approved Go-live (week 6), not the current forecast.

regions.label: Forecast
regions.why: The updated forecast Go-live at week 7, reflecting the slip.

explanation: The baseline milestone sits at week 6 (the approved plan). The forecast milestone has slipped to week 7 — that red diamond is the current forecast completion.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition, Schedule Performance Domain
Process Groups: A Practice Guide, Develop Schedule

prompt: Cost of quality splits into conformance and nonconformance. Tap the bar for the largest cost of NONconformance.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 310 196' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><line x1='44' y1='170' x2='300' y2='170' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><text x='120' y='16' fill='#2F6F5E' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Cost of conformance</text><text x='248' y='16' fill='#B5462F' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Cost of NONconformance</text><rect x='50' y='122' width='46' height='48' rx='3' stroke='#2F6F5E' stroke-width='1' fill='#2F6F5E' fill-opacity='0.5'/><text x='73' y='182' fill='currentColor' font-size='7' text-anchor='middle'>Prevention</text><text x='73' y='119' fill='currentColor' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>8</text><rect x='110' y='110' width='46' height='60' rx='3' stroke='#2F6F5E' stroke-width='1' fill='#2F6F5E' fill-opacity='0.5'/><text x='133' y='182' fill='currentColor' font-size='7' text-anchor='middle'>Appraisal</text><text x='133' y='107' fill='currentColor' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>10</text><rect x='170' y='74' width='46' height='96' rx='3' stroke='#B5462F' stroke-width='1' fill='#B5462F' fill-opacity='0.5'/><text x='193' y='182' fill='currentColor' font-size='7' text-anchor='middle'>Internal</text><text x='193' y='192' fill='currentColor' font-size='7' text-anchor='middle'>failure</text><text x='193' y='71' fill='currentColor' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>16</text><rect x='230' y='38' width='46' height='132' rx='3' stroke='#B5462F' stroke-width='1' fill='#B5462F' fill-opacity='0.5'/><text x='253' y='182' fill='currentColor' font-size='7' text-anchor='middle'>External</text><text x='253' y='192' fill='currentColor' font-size='7' text-anchor='middle'>failure</text><text x='253' y='35' fill='currentColor' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>22</text></svg>
figure.caption: Green = conformance; red = nonconformance.

regions.label: Prevention
regions.why: A cost of conformance (prevention/appraisal), not failure.

regions.label: Appraisal
regions.why: A cost of conformance (prevention/appraisal), not failure.

regions.label: Internal failure
regions.why: A nonconformance cost, but smaller than external failure.

regions.label: External failure
regions.why: External failure — a cost of NONconformance (failure reaching the customer); the costliest bucket here.

explanation: Prevention and appraisal are costs of conformance. Internal and external failure are costs of nonconformance; external failure (reaching the customer) is the largest and most damaging here.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition, Quality Performance Domain
Process Groups: A Practice Guide, Plan Quality Management

prompt: This scatter diagram shows a positive trend. Tap the outlier — the point that does NOT fit the pattern.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 320 196' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><line x1='44' y1='172' x2='300' y2='172' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><line x1='44' y1='26' x2='44' y2='172' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><text x='172' y='185' fill='currentColor' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>review hours →</text><circle cx='69.6' cy='145.5' r='3.2' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='95.2' cy='129.5' r='3.2' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='120.8' cy='117.6' r='3.2' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='146.4' cy='101.7' r='3.2' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='172' cy='92.4' r='3.2' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='197.6' cy='76.4' r='3.2' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='223.2' cy='68.5' r='3.2' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='248.8' cy='52.5' r='3.2' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='274.4' cy='43.3' r='3.2' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='184.8' cy='152.1' r='3.2' stroke='#B5462F' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#B5462F' fill-opacity='1'/></svg>
figure.caption: Each dot = one review.

regions.label: Outlier
regions.why: This point breaks the upward trend (high review time but very few defects) — the outlier.
On the main trend line — not an outlier.
On the main trend line — not an outlier.

explanation: Most points rise together (more review time, more defects found). One point sits far off that trend — high review time yet very few defects — the outlier worth investigating.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition, Quality Performance Domain
Process Groups: A Practice Guide, Manage Quality

prompt: Tap the histogram bar that falls outside the specification limits (LSL/USL).

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 310 196' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><line x1='44' y1='170' x2='300' y2='170' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><line x1='48' y1='26' x2='48' y2='170' stroke='#B5462F' stroke-width='1.2' stroke-dasharray='5 3'/><text x='48' y='22' fill='#B5462F' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>LSL</text><line x1='262' y1='26' x2='262' y2='170' stroke='#B5462F' stroke-width='1.2' stroke-dasharray='5 3'/><text x='262' y='22' fill='#B5462F' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>USL</text><rect x='50' y='143' width='28' height='27' rx='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='0.55'/><rect x='86' y='107' width='28' height='63' rx='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='0.55'/><rect x='122' y='62' width='28' height='108' rx='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='0.55'/><rect x='158' y='35' width='28' height='135' rx='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='0.55'/><rect x='194' y='71' width='28' height='99' rx='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='0.55'/><rect x='230' y='116' width='28' height='54' rx='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='0.55'/><rect x='266' y='89' width='28' height='81' rx='3' stroke='#B5462F' stroke-width='1' fill='#B5462F' fill-opacity='0.55'/></svg>
figure.caption: Dashed red = specification limits.

regions.label: bar 1
regions.why: Within the spec limits.

regions.label: bar 2
regions.why: Within the spec limits.

regions.label: bar 3
regions.why: Within the spec limits.

regions.label: bar 4
regions.why: Within the spec limits.

regions.label: bar 5
regions.why: Within the spec limits.

regions.label: bar 6
regions.why: Within the spec limits.

regions.label: bar 7
regions.why: This bar falls beyond the upper spec limit (USL) — out of specification.

explanation: The dashed red lines are the lower and upper spec limits. Six bars sit inside them; the final bar lies beyond the USL — out of specification and a quality concern.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition, Quality Performance Domain
Process Groups: A Practice Guide, Control Quality

prompt: On this run chart, tap the stretch that signals a non-random pattern (seven points on one side of the mean).

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 320 196' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><line x1='44' y1='172' x2='300' y2='172' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><line x1='44' y1='24' x2='44' y2='172' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><line x1='44' y1='98' x2='300' y2='98' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.2'/><text x='300' y='95' fill='currentColor' font-size='8' text-anchor='end'>mean</text><polyline points='56,122 79.6,66 103.2,114 126.8,78 150.4,74 174,66 197.6,70 221.2,62 244.8,74 268.4,66 292,118' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.8'/><circle cx='56' cy='122' r='2.6' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='79.6' cy='66' r='2.6' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='103.2' cy='114' r='2.6' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='126.8' cy='78' r='2.6' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='150.4' cy='74' r='2.6' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='174' cy='66' r='2.6' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='197.6' cy='70' r='2.6' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='221.2' cy='62' r='2.6' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='244.8' cy='74' r='2.6' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='268.4' cy='66' r='2.6' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='292' cy='118' r='2.6' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/></svg>
figure.caption: Solid line = mean.

regions.label: The run
regions.why: Seven consecutive points fall on the same side of the mean — a non-random 'run' (rule of seven).

regions.label: Early points
regions.why: These cross the mean — normal variation, not a run.

regions.label: Last point
regions.why: A single point back below the mean — not the run.

explanation: The rule of seven: seven or more consecutive points on one side of the mean indicate a non-random shift, even with no control limits breached. Tap that run of points above the mean.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition, Quality Performance Domain
Process Groups: A Practice Guide, Control Quality

prompt: On this fishbone diagram, tap the category branch where an 'equipment out of calibration' cause belongs.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 340 196' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><line x1='30' y1='110' x2='300' y2='110' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.6'/><polygon points='300,103 314,110 300,117' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1' fill='currentColor' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='316' y='114' fill='currentColor' font-size='8' text-anchor='start'>Effect</text><line x1='70' y1='50' x2='116' y2='110' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.2'/><text x='70' y='54' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>People</text><line x1='150' y1='50' x2='196' y2='110' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.2'/><text x='150' y='54' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Process</text><line x1='110' y1='170' x2='156' y2='110' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.2'/><text x='110' y='174' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Materials</text><line x1='200' y1='170' x2='246' y2='110' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.2'/><text x='200' y='174' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Machines</text></svg>
figure.caption: Branches = cause categories; arrow = the effect.

regions.label: People
regions.why: The People category — not where equipment causes go.

regions.label: Process
regions.why: The Process category — not where equipment causes go.

regions.label: Materials
regions.why: The Materials category — not where equipment causes go.

regions.label: Machines
regions.why: Machines/equipment category — where an equipment-calibration cause belongs.

explanation: A fishbone groups causes by category (People, Process, Materials, Machines, ...). An equipment-calibration problem is a Machines/equipment cause, so it goes on that branch.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition, Quality Performance Domain
Process Groups: A Practice Guide, Manage Quality

prompt: On this tornado diagram, tap the variable the project outcome is MOST sensitive to.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 350 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><line x1='175' y1='24' x2='175' y2='176' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><text x='175' y='18' fill='currentColor' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>baseline</text><rect x='85' y='36' width='180' height='26' rx='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='0.4'/><text x='175' y='53' fill='currentColor' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Material price</text><rect x='111' y='74' width='128' height='26' rx='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='0.4'/><text x='175' y='91' fill='currentColor' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Labor rate</text><rect x='131' y='112' width='88' height='26' rx='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='0.4'/><text x='175' y='129' fill='currentColor' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Scope volatility</text><rect x='149' y='150' width='52' height='26' rx='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='0.4'/><text x='175' y='167' fill='currentColor' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>FX rate</text></svg>
figure.caption: Bars sorted widest-first around the baseline.

regions.label: Material price
regions.why: The widest bar — the variable the outcome is MOST sensitive to.

regions.label: Labor rate
regions.why: A narrower bar — less impact on the outcome.

regions.label: Scope volatility
regions.why: A narrower bar — less impact on the outcome.

regions.label: FX rate
regions.why: A narrower bar — less impact on the outcome.

explanation: A tornado diagram ranks variables by how much they swing the outcome, widest at the top. The longest bar (material price) has the greatest impact, so it is the most sensitive variable.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition, Uncertainty Performance Domain
Process Groups: A Practice Guide, Perform Quantitative Risk Analysis

prompt: On this salience model, tap the DEFINITIVE stakeholder (the highest-priority one).

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 220' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><circle cx='130' cy='100' r='60' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='200' cy='100' r='60' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='165' cy='158' r='60' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='95' y='70' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>Power</text><text x='235' y='70' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>Legitimacy</text><text x='165' y='200' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>Urgency</text><circle cx='165' cy='119.3' r='5' stroke='#2F6F5E' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#2F6F5E' fill-opacity='1'/><circle cx='165' cy='82' r='4' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/></svg>
figure.caption: Three overlapping attributes: power, legitimacy, urgency.

regions.label: Definitive
regions.why: Sits in all three circles — power + legitimacy + urgency = a definitive stakeholder (highest priority).

regions.label: Dominant
regions.why: Only power + legitimacy (no urgency) = dominant, not definitive.

regions.label: Power only
regions.why: Only power = a dormant stakeholder.

explanation: The salience model classifies stakeholders by power, legitimacy, and urgency. A stakeholder with all three — in the central overlap — is 'definitive' and the highest priority for engagement.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition, Stakeholders Performance Domain
Process Groups: A Practice Guide, Identify Stakeholders

prompt: RACI rule: exactly one 'A' (Accountable) per row. Tap the row that BREAKS this rule.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 340 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><text x='135' y='24' fill='currentColor' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>PM</text><text x='185' y='24' fill='currentColor' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>Sponsor</text><text x='235' y='24' fill='currentColor' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>Team</text><text x='285' y='24' fill='currentColor' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>QA</text><text x='104' y='50' fill='currentColor' font-size='9' text-anchor='end'>Charter</text><rect x='110' y='30' width='50' height='32' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.7' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='135' y='50' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>R</text><rect x='160' y='30' width='50' height='32' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.7' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='185' y='50' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>A</text><rect x='210' y='30' width='50' height='32' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.7' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='235' y='50' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>C</text><rect x='260' y='30' width='50' height='32' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.7' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='285' y='50' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>I</text><text x='104' y='82' fill='currentColor' font-size='9' text-anchor='end'>Plan</text><rect x='110' y='62' width='50' height='32' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.7' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='135' y='82' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>A</text><rect x='160' y='62' width='50' height='32' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.7' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='185' y='82' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>A</text><rect x='210' y='62' width='50' height='32' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.7' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='235' y='82' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>R</text><rect x='260' y='62' width='50' height='32' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.7' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='285' y='82' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>I</text><text x='104' y='114' fill='currentColor' font-size='9' text-anchor='end'>Build</text><rect x='110' y='94' width='50' height='32' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.7' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='135' y='114' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>A</text><rect x='160' y='94' width='50' height='32' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.7' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='185' y='114' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>I</text><rect x='210' y='94' width='50' height='32' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.7' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='235' y='114' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>R</text><rect x='260' y='94' width='50' height='32' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.7' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='285' y='114' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>C</text></svg>
figure.caption: R responsible · A accountable · C consulted · I informed.

regions.label: Charter row
regions.why: Valid: exactly one A and at least one R.

regions.label: Plan row
regions.why: Two people are Accountable in this row — a RACI rule violation (exactly one A per row).

regions.label: Build row
regions.why: Valid: exactly one A and at least one R.

explanation: Each row must have exactly one Accountable. The 'Plan' row lists two A's (PM and Sponsor), which violates RACI — accountability must rest with a single person.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition, Team Performance Domain
Process Groups: A Practice Guide, Plan Resource Management

prompt: On this conflict-mode grid (assertiveness vs cooperativeness), tap the win-win 'collaborate' mode.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 220' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><rect x='60' y='26' width='230' height='170' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><line x1='175' y1='26' x2='175' y2='196' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.6'/><line x1='60' y1='111' x2='290' y2='111' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.6'/><text x='56' y='111' fill='currentColor' font-size='7' text-anchor='end'>Assertiveness</text><text x='175' y='210' fill='currentColor' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Cooperativeness →</text><text x='117.5' y='72.5' fill='currentColor' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>Compete</text><text x='232.5' y='72.5' fill='#2F6F5E' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>Collaborate</text><text x='117.5' y='157.5' fill='currentColor' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>Avoid</text><text x='232.5' y='157.5' fill='currentColor' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>Accommodate</text><text x='175' y='105' fill='currentColor' font-size='7' text-anchor='middle'>Compromise</text></svg>
figure.caption: Quadrants by assertiveness (y) and cooperativeness (x).

regions.label: Compete
regions.why: High assertiveness, low cooperation — force/win-lose.

regions.label: Collaborate
regions.why: High assertiveness + high cooperation — the win-win, problem-solving mode.

regions.label: Avoid
regions.why: Low on both — withdrawing.

regions.label: Accommodate
regions.why: Low assertiveness, high cooperation — smoothing.

explanation: Thomas-Kilmann maps conflict styles by assertiveness and cooperativeness. High on both is Collaborate (confronting / problem-solving) — the win-win mode PMI generally favors for important issues.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition, Team Performance Domain
Process Groups: A Practice Guide, Manage Team

prompt: Tap the Tuckman stage where team performance typically DIPS before it improves.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 320 192' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><line x1='44' y1='168' x2='300' y2='168' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><line x1='44' y1='30' x2='44' y2='168' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3'/><text x='40' y='34' fill='currentColor' font-size='7' text-anchor='end'>perf.</text><polyline points='58,99 115,140.4 172,85.2 229,36.9 286,112.8' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='58' cy='99' r='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='58' y='180' fill='currentColor' font-size='7' text-anchor='middle'>Forming</text><circle cx='115' cy='140.4' r='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='115' y='180' fill='currentColor' font-size='7' text-anchor='middle'>Storming</text><circle cx='172' cy='85.2' r='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='172' y='180' fill='currentColor' font-size='7' text-anchor='middle'>Norming</text><circle cx='229' cy='36.9' r='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='229' y='180' fill='currentColor' font-size='7' text-anchor='middle'>Performing</text><circle cx='286' cy='112.8' r='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1.3' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='1'/><text x='286' y='180' fill='currentColor' font-size='7' text-anchor='middle'>Adjourning</text></svg>
figure.caption: Curve = team performance over the stages.

regions.label: Forming
regions.why: Forming — not the dip stage.

regions.label: Storming
regions.why: Storming — conflict emerges as roles are negotiated and performance dips before it recovers.

regions.label: Norming
regions.why: Norming — not the dip stage.

regions.label: Performing
regions.why: Performing — not the dip stage.

regions.label: Adjourning
regions.why: Adjourning — not the dip stage.

explanation: Tuckman's model runs Forming, Storming, Norming, Performing, Adjourning. Performance typically dips during Storming as members surface conflict and negotiate roles, then climbs through Norming into Performing.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition, Team Performance Domain
Process Groups: A Practice Guide, Manage Team

prompt: On this cumulative flow diagram, tap the band that reveals a bottleneck (work piling up).

figure.caption: Stacked bands: Done, WIP, To-do over time.

regions.label: WIP band
regions.why: The In-Progress (WIP) band keeps widening — work arrives faster than it completes: a bottleneck.

regions.label: Done band
regions.why: The Done band grows steadily — not the bottleneck.

regions.label: To-do band
regions.why: Backlog shrinking normally — not the bottleneck.

explanation: In a CFD, a widening band means arrivals outpace completions. The In-Progress (WIP) band keeps expanding while Done grows slowly — a clear bottleneck to address.
references: Agile Practice Guide, Kanban and Flow
PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition, Delivery Performance Domain

prompt: Each column shows its WIP limit. Tap the column that has BREACHED its work-in-progress limit.

fill-opacity='1'/><text x='241' y='26' fill='currentColor' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>Test</text><text x='241' y='36' fill='currentColor' font-size='7' text-anchor='middle'>WIP 3</text><rect stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='0.4'/><rect x='284' y='158' width='42' height='16' rx='3' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='1' fill='#1F5FA8' fill-opacity='0.4'/></svg>
figure.caption: Cards stacked per column; WIP = the column limit.

regions.label: Analysis
regions.why: Within its WIP limit.

regions.label: Dev
regions.why: Holds more cards than its WIP limit — the over-limit bottleneck column.

regions.label: Test
regions.why: Within its WIP limit.

explanation: Kanban caps work-in-progress per column. The Dev column holds 5 cards against a WIP limit of 3 — it has breached its limit and is the flow bottleneck to clear before pulling more work.
references: Agile Practice Guide, Kanban and Flow
PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition, Delivery Performance Domain

prompt: In this stakeholder engagement matrix, C = current and D = desired. Tap the stakeholder with the LARGEST current-to-desired gap.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 340 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><text x='130' y='32' fill='currentColor' font-size='6' text-anchor='middle'>Unaware</text><text x='170' y='32' font-size='8' text-anchor='end'>End users</text><rect x='110' y='100' width='40' height='30' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.5' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><rect x='150' y='100' width='40' height='30' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.5' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><rect x='190' y='100' width='40' height='30' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.5' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.5' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><rect x='150' y='130' width='40' height='30' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.5' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><rect x='190' y='130' width='40' height='30' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.5' fill='none' fill-opacity='1'/><rect x='230' y='130' width='40' height='30' rx='3' stroke='currentColor' text-anchor='middle'>C = current · D = desired</text></svg>
figure.caption: Cells: Unaware → Leading; C current, D desired.

regions.label: Sponsor
regions.why: C-to-D gap of 1 — smaller than the Regulator's.

regions.label: Regulator
regions.why: Largest gap between current (C) and desired (D) engagement — needs the most attention.

regions.label: End users
regions.why: C-to-D gap of 0 — smaller than the Regulator's.

regions.label: Vendor
regions.why: C-to-D gap of 0 — smaller than the Regulator's.

explanation: The engagement assessment matrix plots each stakeholder's current (C) versus desired (D) engagement. The Regulator must move from Unaware to Neutral — the biggest gap — so it needs the most engagement effort.
references: PMBOK Guide Eighth Edition, Stakeholders Performance Domain
Process Groups: A Practice Guide, Plan Stakeholder Engagement
